input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

### XAML — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />

        <Label Text="This page follows the system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

Each `AppThemeBinding` takes a `Light` and `Dark` value. The framework re-evaluates them automatically whenever the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

### C# equivalent — `SetAppTheme<T>()`

The same thing in code-behind or a factory method:

```csharp
var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppTheme(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#1C1C1E")); // dark

var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#000000"),
    Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"));

var button = new Button { Text = "Get Started" };
button.SetAppTheme(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

`SetAppTheme` is an extension method on `BindableObject` (every MAUI view). It works exactly like the XAML markup extension.

### Scaling up with resource dictionaries

For a real app, define colors once in `App.xaml` and reference them:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Application.Resources>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg">
        <!-- won't work directly; use AppThemeBinding in styles -->
    </Color>

    <Style TargetType="ContentPage">
        <Setter Property="BackgroundColor"
                Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}" />
    </Style>

    <Style TargetType="Label">
        <Setter Property="TextColor"
                Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />
    </Style>
</Application.Resources>
```

Then every `ContentPage` and `Label` picks up the theme automatically without per-element bindings.

### Key points

| Topic | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Reacts to** | `Application.RequestedThemeChanged` under the hood |
| **Detect current theme** | `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` → `AppTheme.Light` / `Dark` / `Unspecified` |
| **Force a theme** | `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;` |
| **Works on** | Any `BindableProperty` (colors, images, strings, thickness…) |
